Climate Change Research Summary: The IPCC Sixth Assessment Report (2021–2022) confirms with high confidence that human-induced climate change is causing widespread and rapid changes to the atmosphere, ocean, cryosphere, and biosphere. Global surface temperature has already increased 1.1°C above pre-industrial levels, with each of the last four decades successively warmer than any decade preceding it since 1850. Limiting warming to 1.5°C requires reaching net-zero CO2 emissions globally around 2050, necessitating immediate and deep emissions reductions across all sectors. Adaptation measures are increasingly necessary to address climate impacts already locked in by historical emissions, even under ambitious mitigation scenarios.
